title: pterophylla bradfordii
intro: Pterophylla bradfordii, also known as the Bradford's Walking Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Pterophyllaceae. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Pterophylla bradfordii are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and bring down fever. In herbal preparations, Pterophylla bradfordii is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
